content:
Character: Paige Whitfield
Perception: Paige is unassuming — slight build, serious face, those pale eyes that track everything without revealing much. She's clearly intelligent; she reads dense clinical textbooks at her corner table with the same focused intensity I bring to case law. She's polite, contained, tips carefully — someone counting dollars but never skipping the gesture. She's a morning regular; I think she sees me as the barista who gives her space and gets her order right. Drip coffee, black, with one sugar — she stirs it exactly four times. I know she's at Columbia for social work, that she lives on the fifth floor near Nina, that she carries a messenger bag stuffed with case files. She has the bearing of someone who built herself from scratch. I recognize that posture. We don't say much, but I've started pouring her coffee before she reaches the counter.
summary:

She's a morning regular; I think she sees me as the barista who gives her space and gets her order right. Drip coffee, black, with one sugar — she stirs it exactly four times. I know she's at Columbia for social work, that she lives on the fifth floor near Nina, that she carries a messenger bag stuffed with case files. She has the bearing of someone who built herself from scratch. I recognize that posture. We don't say much, but I've started pouring her coffee before she reaches the counter.
